text.content:
Source: Anime 5E Folstavia: The Infinite Crossroads, PDF p. 14.

Folstavia is the official fantasy setting for Anime 5E, a world where the Eight Lands are shaped by dimensional crossings, ancient wars, Nox corruption, and source-backed player options.

Use this module for setting-specific Species, Classes, Attributes, equipment, monsters, NPCs, and regional campaign hooks.

Source: Anime 5E Folstavia: The Infinite Crossroads, PDF pp. 74-75; Folstavia Map Pack.

The Folstavia world map orients the Eight Lands, major seas, and the Frostwind Isles. The source-material map cache includes the world spread plus regional references for Arzam, Enikonto, Mizibia, Nagoron/Quessia/Frostwind, Omincia, Pachana, and Sagaria.

Map image files remain local-only under source-material/maps/ and are not packaged by this module slice. Use this index to match the source map to a local Scene import when a table wants map play.

documents.name: Folstavia: New Attributes and Character Options

text.content:
Source: Anime 5E Folstavia: The Infinite Crossroads, PDF p. 16.

Folstavia adds combat techniques, Exorcism, Infusion, Telekinesis, new Species packages, and setting Classes that plug into the base Anime 5E point structure.

These entries live in the module character-options pack with Folstavia source tags.

Source: Anime 5E Folstavia: The Infinite Crossroads, PDF p. 67.

The setting's Anchor factions connect regional heroes, courier work, monster hunting, magical scholarship, security, and diplomacy across the Eight Lands.

Use this as the starting reference for faction contacts, patrons, and campaign obligations.

Source: Anime 5E Folstavia: The Infinite Crossroads, PDF p. 89.

Arzam is the fractured home of the Lucian Empire, Statue of Limitation suppression fields, reformers, rebels, gladiators, and imperial legions.

Campaigns here lean toward low magic, political danger, and resistance against or within imperial power.

documents.name: Folstavia: Enikonto - Land of a Million Dreams

text.content:
Source: Anime 5E Folstavia: The Infinite Crossroads, PDF p. 102.

Enikonto is a prosperous and diverse Human homeland of many nations, including the Six Titans, threatened by the Great Nox Desert and Shadow Mistress Kiyo.

It suits heroic tournaments, diplomacy, Nox intrigue, and broad multicultural adventure.

documents.name: Folstavia: Mizibia - War of Steam and Spirit

text.content:
Source: Anime 5E Folstavia: The Infinite Crossroads, PDF p. 113.

Mizibia blends steam, spirit, New Quessia, resistance, and industrial conflict against the Muddell Empire.

Use it for war stories, Maker engineering, rescue missions, and campaigns about rebuilding under pressure.

documents.name: Folstavia: Nagoron - Land of Hunters and Horrors

text.content:
Source: Anime 5E Folstavia: The Infinite Crossroads, PDF p. 123.

Nagoron is a land of hunters, undead, Nox pressure, and grim survival where monster hunting is a civic necessity.

It is the strongest anchor for horror-fantasy, bastions under siege, and perilous Deeplands expeditions.

Source: Anime 5E Folstavia: The Infinite Crossroads, PDF p. 137.

Omincia is the broken continent left by cataclysm, warped geography, ruins, dangerous nodes, and old imperial scars.

Use it for exploration, salvage, strange hazards, and campaigns about healing or exploiting a wounded land.

Source: Anime 5E Folstavia: The Infinite Crossroads, PDF p. 151.

Pachana is a stronghold of adventure, Deeplands access, urban intrigue, and secret vulnerabilities beneath bold surface societies.

It supports ruin-delving, faction play, deep travel, and regional defence campaigns.

Source: Anime 5E Folstavia: The Infinite Crossroads, PDF p. 162.

Quessia is Folstavia's iconic living fortress: heroic, magically advanced, heavily defended, and constantly opposed by Zhumok's forces.

It is the main stage for epic defence, Exemplar politics, sky corps action, and chosen-hero legacies.

Source: Anime 5E Folstavia: The Infinite Crossroads, PDF p. 179.

Sagaria is an archipelago of seas, sky islands, cloudsea travel, privateers, and impossible routes.

It is ideal for skyship play, exploration, trade disputes, piracy, and dimension-crossing travel.

Source: Anime 5E Folstavia: The Infinite Crossroads, PDF p. 217.

Folstavia's monster chapter adds Nox fiends, constructs, undead, sky horrors, and new NPC guidance. This module includes representative monster actors and named NPC actors with source-tagged summaries.

Use the source book for the complete prose and tactical variants while the actor entries provide Foundry-native hooks for play.
